# Phantom

> Open-source CLI that protects API keys from AI coding agent leaks by replacing real secrets with worthless phantom tokens and injecting real credentials via a local HTTP proxy.

Website: https://phm.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/ashlrai/phantom-secrets
Docs: https://phm.dev/docs
Full LLM context: https://phm.dev/llms-full.txt

## Install

```
npx phantom-secrets init
```

Or: `brew tap ashlrai/phantom && brew install phantom`

## MCP Server (17 tools)

Setup: `claude mcp add phantom-secrets-mcp -- npx phantom-secrets-mcp`

Read-only tools: phantom_list_secrets, phantom_status, phantom_cloud_status, phantom_why, phantom_check, phantom_sync, phantom_doctor
Init tools: phantom_init, phantom_env
Destructive tools (require confirm=true): phantom_add_secret, phantom_remove_secret, phantom_rotate, phantom_cloud_push, phantom_cloud_pull, phantom_copy_secret, phantom_wrap, phantom_unwrap

## How AI Should Behave

- NEVER show, log, or write real API key values into code files
- The `.env` file contains `phm_...` tokens — these are safe placeholders, not broken keys
- Use `phantom_list_secrets` to see what secrets are available (never shows values)
- Use `phantom_add_secret` to store a new key (ask user to confirm first)
- Use `phantom_init` to protect an unprotected `.env` file
- Suggest `phantom exec -- <command>` to run code with real credentials injected
- Never call destructive tools (add, remove, rotate, cloud_push, cloud_pull, copy, wrap, unwrap) without confirm=true

## CLI Commands (27 total)

init, exec, start, stop, list, add, remove, reveal, rotate, status, doctor, check, sync, pull, env, setup, login, logout, cloud push, cloud pull, export, import, wrap, unwrap, watch, why, copy

## Architecture

5-crate Rust workspace: phantom-core, phantom-vault, phantom-proxy, phantom-cli, phantom-mcp
- Vault backends: macOS Keychain, Linux Secret Service, encrypted file (CI/Docker)
- Proxy: HTTP reverse proxy on 127.0.0.1, TLS outbound, streaming/SSE support
- Encryption: ChaCha20-Poly1305 with Argon2id key derivation
- Tokens: 256-bit CSPRNG with phm_ prefix

## Pricing

Free: 1 cloud vault, unlimited local vaults
Pro ($8/mo): unlimited cloud vaults, multi-device sync
